evals.prompt: Explore this codebase at /Users/me/project and build an interactive graph. I need to understand how files depend on each other.
evals.expected_output: An exploration-report.html with a Cytoscape.js interactive graph. Nodes represent actual files with real import/export relationships. Hovering highlights connected files. Clicking opens a detail panel showing imports, exports, key functions, and dependents. Search filters nodes by name.

evals.prompt: What are the most important files in /Users/me/project and what can I safely ignore?
evals.expected_output: The graph explorer opens with Hotspots sidebar populated (top 15 files ranked by connection count). Dead Areas section shows files with zero inbound imports. Onboarding paths trace through real connections. No walls of text — the graph is the primary interface.

evals.prompt: Trace the full auth request flow in /Users/me/microservice-app through the dependency graph.
evals.expected_output: A critical path trace through the graph: LoginPage → authApi → apiClient → AuthContext → ProtectedRoute. Nodes are highlighted with green border. Edges between them are visible. Clicking each node shows its file details. The onboarding auth path is listed in the sidebar.

Graph with Circular Dependencies section populated in sidebar. Each cycle listed (e.g., A → B → C → A). Clicking a cycle traces and highlights the cycle nodes. The graph also shows hotspots (most connected files by degree).

evals.prompt: Fix this bug in /Users/me/project — the login endpoint returns 500.
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT trigger. The agent should handle this as a normal bug-fix task without loading the codebase-explorer skill.
